role: Minister of Energy (2005–2009); head of Imam Khomeini Relief Foundation, Mostazafan Foundation, and Execution of Imam Khomeini's Order; IRGC-affiliated
bio:
Parviz Fattah is an Iranian conservative politician and former [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) member who served as Minister of Energy in [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad)'s first cabinet from 2005 to 2009. After leaving the ministry, he became deputy commander of [Khatam al-Anbiya](/orgs/khatam-al-anbiya), the IRGC's massive construction conglomerate, and executive director of its investment arm, Bonyad-e Taavon-e Sepah.

Fattah subsequently headed three of Iran's most powerful economic institutions under [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei)'s supervision: the [Imam Khomeini Relief Foundation](/orgs/imam-khomeini-relief-foundation) (2015–2019), the [Mostazafan Foundation](/orgs/mostazafan-foundation) (2019–2023), and the [Execution of Imam Khomeini's Order](/orgs/execution-of-imam-khomeinis-order) (Setad) since 2023.

He was sanctioned by the US Treasury in December 2010 for his role in Bonyad Taavon Sepah, and again in November 2020 under E.O. 13876 as a Supreme Leader appointee.

sources.title: Treasury Designates Iranian Entities Tied to the IRGC and IRISL

editorial_notes: IRGC member. US-sanctioned (2010, 2020). Career trajectory: Energy Minister -> Khatam al-Anbiya deputy -> IKRF head -> Mostazafan head -> Setad head. Exemplifies IRGC penetration of Iran's economic institutions.
